programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable(n)

programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Halloween presents one of the last big sales opportunities of the year, so the pressure is on Emma and Martin from the produce team, battling the wet weather to ensure this year's pumpkin crop will deliver the one million required for stores around the country. In the Locksbottom branch in Kent, Halloween could end up being a bit of a nightmare. The store has a low customer satisfaction rating, so manager Mij is on a mission to retrain and re-invigorate his young team in preparation for the big pumpkin push. Meanwhile, Laura and Donna from the communications team head to Romania for the filming of Sainsbury's Dickensian-themed Christmas advert. How will it stand up against the commercials produced by their rivals?(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Huw Edwards takes the helm for the BBC's comprehensive overnight coverage as the results of the General Election are announced, joined by Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Faisal Islam and Amol Rajan, along with Professor John Curtice. Sophie Raworth analyses the results on a giant map of the UK, Sarah Smith and Kirsty Wark broadcast live from Scotland, and correspondents including Naga Munchetty, Andrew Marr, Martha Kearney, Nick Robinson and Lucy Manning will have news and results from key constituency locations across the country. Finally, the exit poll - and the eventual result - will be projected onto Old Broadcasting House, providing a clear idea of who will be leading the country over the next five years(n)

programme.title: Election 2019

programme.title: Election 2019

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Northampton, Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to win the right to make over the garden of Theresa and Martyn. Theresa has recently retired from a career in nursing and Martyn has retired from a long teaching career. So, after being married for 42 years, and with their children moved out and providing them with grand children, they are free to enjoy their new 'forever home'. However, their garden is presently 'boring' and 'too green'(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: As the dust settles after the first winter General Election in decades, Fiona Bruce is joined in London by guests from across the political spectrum to ascertain what changes the outcome is likely to bring. The main focus is Brexit, and what changes are most likely for the United Kingdom in terms of its future relationship with the European Union. However, the campaign also turned up a lot of other issues regarding the NHS, public spending and defence, and all these matters are likely to be debated in this special edition(n)

programme.title: Fleabag

Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. There's tough talking, turmoil and tears as bravado breaks, egos are eradicated and business plans bulldozed, before Alan decides which two candidates have done enough to earn a place in the final(n)

Doctors said he would be in a wheelchair within a year, but instead Doddie has battled the disease and is on a mission to help find a cure. Fellow player John Beattie has followed Doddie and his family over the past two years, gaining a unique insight into the life and mission of the man behind the famous tartan suit(n)

This week's contestants are mother and daughter Tracey and Drea from High Wycombe, married couple Daniel and Rebecca from Aberdare, and brothers Karl and Ivan from Manchester. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

As usual Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse will be reviewing their efforts, but only the viewers' votes will count to determine the winner. There is also a performance by pop superstar Taylor Swift(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show

programme.title: Match of the Day

Jurgen Klopp's Reds held an extremely strong recent record over the Hornets, with their last three meetings on this ground finishing 5-0, 5-0 and 6-1. The home fans will have been hoping for more of the same to maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title. Plus, action from Burnley v Newcastle United at Turf Moor, Chelsea v Bournemouth at Stamford Bridge, Sheffield United v Aston Villa at Bramall Lane and Southampton v West Ham United at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives, focusing on dishes that all have something of a kick to them(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by DJ Dev Griffin as they rustle up roast garlic-infused baked camembert, blueberry cinnamon swirls and spicy vanilla rice pudding(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio are joined by Debbie McGee as they rustle up slow-cooked duck, a duo of soups and warm chocolate cake with sweet clementine cheese(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor and writer Mark Gatiss has always had a love of storytelling, especially for the ghastly and the ghoulish, but has always wondered where it comes from. Setting out to research his ancestry, he is intrigued to discover that his mother's side of the family goes back five generations to the wilds of Northern Ireland. Travelling over there, he untangles a tale of rags-to-riches and learns that sure enough, his family were indeed story-tellers - but fascinatingly, they were also said to be vampire-slayers(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Denise Lewis joins the duo as they create a trio of canapes, porcetta pork loin and a prawn and salmon terrine. The retired athlete also explains how she spends her Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun there are also fantastic ideas for homemade edible Christmas presents(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the finalists' friends and family, who share their thoughts on how their loved ones will do, and designer Vicky Gill reveals some of Saturday night's costumes(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Russell Kane and presenter AJ Odudu navigate their way around Shropshire looking for antiques, accompanied by experts Phil Serrell and Margie Cooper. Eventually they head to an auction to discover whose haul raises the most money(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The last semi-final sees the remaining hopefuls head to Ollie Dabbous's London restaurant Hide, where they receive a masterclass from the Michelin-starred chef in making his beef tartare. He then asks them to create a dish from the restaurant's select larder before they are each given responsibility for one of the dishes during a packed service. Back at the studio, a final test awaits in which the hopefuls must cook two dishes for Gregg Wallace, Marcus Wareing and Monica Galetti, who decide who will go through to next week's finals(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig has a Christmas knees-up in the pub with Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Josh Widdicombe and Noel Fielding. Pub-themed questions are the order of the day, with a sprinkling of festive cheer along the way(n)

programme.title: I Give It a Year (2013)
programme.desc: A couple marry after a whirlwind romance, but family and friends have doubts about the relationship. As their first anniversary approaches, they both begin to wonder if they are right for each other, especially when faced with more appealing alternative partners. Romantic comedy, with Rose Byrne, Rafe Spall, Anna Faris and Simon Baker(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Cheshire to create a piece for a mystery buyer which will mark a new time in their life, being recently single and moving home(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Newly qualified nurse Chloe is thrown in at the deep end treating a toddler who has difficulty breathing. A nightshift worker is flown in showing similar symptoms to a heart attack(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a smashed piggybank, a homemade gramophone that has lost its voice and a tarnished 19th-century German ceremonial helmet(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox delivers a collection of love letters to George, a 24-year-old vet in Hay-on-Wye who has not had a relationship in more than two years. She helps him to arrange a speed dating evening with the writers of his favourite letters - but meeting them face to face proves nerve-racking. Dairy farmer David invites three potential partners to stay with him in Cumbria so they can get a taste of rural life. Plus, an update on Martin and Grace, to see how they are getting on in their dating journeys(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A keeper at Longleat House and Safari Park discovers a concerning abscess on one of her red pandas. After antibiotics and painkillers fail to help the growth, Koda needs an urgent visit to the vet for an operation. Presented by Ben Fogle and Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It's a tale of two flats for Dion Dublin as he visits Sunbury-on-Thames, Surrey, and Sutton Coldfield in the West Midlands. The programme also returns to a mews cottage in Whitstable, Kent, that Lucy Alexander first visited in 2013(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Crews tend to a 95-year-old Navy veteran as he is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. He's diabetic, dehydrated and has suspected acute pancreatitis. Meanwhile. a man has his holiday interrupted with kidney problems. Narrated by Kevin Whately(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs discover what people think of their items, with customers unboxing, testing and scoring a smart toothbrush, eco-friendly cleaning products and a baby change mat. However, only one can win the Customer Is Always Right trophy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents a double bill of contests from Wetherby racecourse in West Yorkshire, with experts Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss helping the two sets of teams. Anita also pays a visit to where the modern railway all began(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for party food, including Lorraine Pascale's chocolate lollipops, seafood dishes by James Martin and Rachel Khoo's coq-au-vin on a stick(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create a Turkey jambalaya, a spicy lamb shank and a madeira cake with a chocolate, chilli and ginger fondue, as they find out how guest Nicki Chapman spends her Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun, there are also fantastic ideas for home-made edible Christmas presents(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Emily Maitlis presents all the results and reaction from the 2019 General Election, with Clive Myrie in Downing Street, Christian Fraser covering analysis of the results, and Jane Hill presenting the latest news from across the country(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal analyse the pro-celebrity couples' progress on the eve of the final. The finalists chat with the hosts ahead of the biggest dance of their lives(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Dane Baptiste, Richard Herring, June Sarpong and Kate Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, as a House of Champions winner is crowned(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills contenders on chosen subjects the history of medicine, the life and works of American singer-songwriter Taylor Swift, the films of Michelangelo Antonioni and the first 50 years of US sporting occasion the Super Bowl. This is followed by a two-minute general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Cocker Spaniel Tilly has been in labour for 12 hours and is struggling to give birth. It's a potentially life-threatening situation, so vet Maurice King decides to perform an urgent C-section to deliver a valuable litter of pups. A young jackdaw is brought into the clinic unable to fly. The vets rehome him with a local woman who has Asperger's syndrome, who has a particular affinity with animals. At the Downpatrick clinic Dexter - a therapy dog at the local hospital - is brought in to see vet Rachael Frew. Dexter has had cancerous lumps on his skin before and more have recently appeared(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: As the monks crowd around Malachia's body, William questions who killed this man since Bernardo had cleverly found and confined the murderer of the other monks. A seemingly unconcerned Bernardo retorts that he had not claimed to have confined the law or the criminals at large in the abbey and warns that only the pope will decide the fate of the Franciscans. William tells Adso that Bernardo with burn Remigio and his pyre will illuminate the encounter between Michele of Cesena - minister general of the Franciscans - and the pope. John Turturro and Rupert Everett star(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regular contributor Hugh Dennis bring the current series towards a close with a special edition of the topical comedy show featuring best bits and unseen material(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett and Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Marguerite (2015)
programme.desc: A wealthy woman in 1920s Paris is a terrible singer, a fact her family strives to prevent her from realising. She gives performances to a small circle of invited audiences, maintaining her delusions of talent - but the situation becomes far more complicated when she decides to perform to the public in an opera house. Period drama, starring Catherine Frot and Andre Marcon. In French, Italian and English(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and Isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For generations, governments have sought to simplify the benefits system. The latest attempt, Universal Credit, merged six benefits into one and was supposed to make life better for claimants - many of them vulnerable. But it's been controversial from the start. Last year, Panorama went to Flintshire in North Wales, one of the first areas to see it rolled out, to reveal the system was creating hardship. Now, the government has made some changes, so Reporter Catrin Nye is returning. Was what she saw a year ago just teething problems, or are there fundamental problems with the new benefit?(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Join Lindsey, Richie and Henry for our big Blue Peter Christmas special! There's an amazing performance from the cast of hit musical The Boy in The Dress, a guest appearance from The Grinch and a spectacular 200 strong kids choir. Plus Christmas comes early for a group of fans who are awarded their Blue Peter badges in festive style; and our famous advent crown lights up the studio once again(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia reveals a harrowing past and Poppy takes over the show(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and her friends hold the series wrap party in the flat(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team search for lethal beasts in Mexico. In the Yucatan Peninsula, he has an encounter with a deadly bull shark, then chases the fastest fish in the sea - the sailfish. Heading inland, Steve is awestruck by the hunting power of a million-strong army of bats as they take to the night sky and finally ventures into a dark cave of hanging serpents - the cold-blooded night snake that can catch a bat in mid-flight(n)

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Pirate Fairy (2014)
programme.desc: A rebellious fairy steals the magic pixie dust that grants her people the power of flight and runs away, becoming captain of a band of pirates. Tinker Bell and her friends set off to recover the dust, but succumb to a spell that switches their magical powers. Disney animated adventure, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Christina Hendricks and Tom Hiddleston(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the third heat. Eight teams battle it out to win a single place in the grand final, pitting robots Foxic, Expulsion, Heavy Metal, MR Speed Squared, Thor, Tauron, Chimera 2 and Concussion against each other in the arena. The show begins with two group battles, followed by a head-to-head between the two overall winners(n)

programme.title: Snow Babies
programme.desc: Documentary following all manner of baby animals born in some of the coldest and harshest places on Earth. Cameras follow the ups and downs of plucky emperor penguins, snow monkeys, polar bears, arctic foxes, reindeer and otters to find out what it takes to survive the first year of life in a world of snow and ice, with a little help from family and friends. Narrated by Caroline Quentin(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first round of semi-finals week. The eight remaining contestants face two challenges, to be judged by Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing. Firstly, they must prepare a dish that captures the moment that triggered their own love of food. After this, they move on to the invention test, in which they must make a flawless vegan meal(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three remaining semi-finalists battle it out for a place in final, and are given the chance to work alongside one of the UK's most exciting chefs, Gareth Ward. First, the chefs are challenged to create a dish of their own design for Gareth to taste, inspired by his own cooking. The competitors will then take on the daunting task of joining Gareth's brigade, taking responsibility for two dishes each during a highly-pressurised service, before returning to the MasterChef kitchen to present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella offers her tips for relaxed entertaining during the festive season, producing dishes including roast duck with orange, soy and ginger, garlic and parmesan mashed potato gratin and sour-sweet soused red cabbage with cranberries. There's also an exotic take on brussel sprouts, a sticky toffee pudding and chocolate pistachio meringue cookies(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in St Albans - including a country lodge, a 1970s house, and an ultra-modern family home - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Robshaws time-travel through six decades of festive nostalgia, beginning by experiencing what the holiday season was like in the 1940s, '50s and '60s. A wartime Christmas has them embracing the make-do and mend spirit for presents and sitting down to ox heart for dinner, with a pudding made primarily of grated potato. The 1950s brings ham with tinned peaches, canapes, cocktails and the first televised Queen's speech, while youngest child Fred receives a 1960s gift of a chocolate smoking set. Giles Coren and Polly Russell present(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part two of two. During their trip from the 1970s to the 1990s, the Robshaws discover how the race for a Christmas number one single was born. They also meet an EastEnders star who reminisces about the soap's tradition of festive misery, and examine Christmas gluttony in the 1990s(n)

programme.title: Nativity! (2009)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher is given the job of directing a nativity play and is determined to outdo a rival from a nearby private school. When he dishonestly claims his ex-girlfriend is a Hollywood producer and is coming to see the show with an eye on making a film, he finds himself becoming a local celebrity. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman and Ashley Jensen(n)

programme.title: Reindeer Family & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan travels to Finnish Lapland to meet a family from the nomadic Sami culture, who rely on reindeer to help them travel across the frozen wilderness. He learns about the culture's traditional bond with the animals and picks up some tips on surviving during a harsh winter, before he and his reindeer companion undertake a gruelling journey to see the northern lights(n)

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like(n)

programme.title: Rod Stewart: Reel Stories
programme.desc: The singer talks to Dermot O'Leary about his life. From Abbey Road to the Royal Albert Hall and Motown to Maggie May, Dermot takes Rod back to his childhood cinema to relive moments from a career spanning six decades(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: The veteran rocker invites Alan Yentob to his LA home to conduct an interview, in which Stewart looks back on a career spanning almost 50 years. He discusses working with acts like the Steampacket and Jeff Beck in the 1960s and his time with the Faces, whose offstage antics became legendary - as did the frontman's affairs with a string of famous women. His solo career propelled him to superstardom with songs such as Maggie May and Sailing, and while the 1980s proved to be less successful, his constant reinvention has given him a lasting career. Having more recently become known as a singer of American standards, he talks about recording Time, his first album of new material in almost 20 years(n)

programme.title: Rod Stewart Live at Hyde Park
programme.desc: A concert by the singer that closed Radio 2's annual Festival in a Day, held in September 2015 in London's Hyde Park. Featuring a selection of hits from his back catalogue including Angel, In a Broken Dream, The Killing of Georgie (Part I and II) as well as Faces classics including Ooh La La and blues standard Rollin' and Tumblin'(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Alumni from Exeter University take on Birmingham University, with TV producer and executive Paul Jackson, news correspondent Jon Kay, comedian Chris Addison and actor Nigel Lindsay among the contestants. Presented by Jeremy Paxman(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox considers whether the building blocks of life might be found on other planets by studying the required substance that is most abundant on Earth - water. He descends to the bottom of the Pacific to discover the creatures that have evolved in this most alien of environments, and flies above the American Scablands to see the effect of the geological changes caused by water in exactly the circumstances astronomers believe occurred on Mars(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the popularity of train replicas, and how what started out as an adult obsession with building fully engineered model railways became the iconic toy of 1950s and 1960s childhood. With contributions from modellers such as Pete Waterman, this programme celebrates the joys of miniaturisation(n)

programme.title: The Secret Story of Stuff: Materials of the Modern Age
programme.desc: Engineer and designer Zoe Laughlin explores the world of material science, uncovering the fascinating innovations in manufacturing that are set to change the world we live in(n)

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: Wildlife in America's mountain regions, including the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada, areas infamous for extremes of temperature from freezing winters to scorching summers. The programme examines the survival strategies employed by animals including black bears, pikas and bison, as well as parasitic plants. Plus, a look at the challenges faced by these region's human inhabitants, including ranchers leading their cattle on 70-mile journeys(n)

programme.title: Oceans Apart: Art and the Pacific with James Fox
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox explores how New Zealand's indigenous Maori people resisted colonisation and marginalisation, and maintained their distinctive culture, so much so that it is now an integral part of modern New Zealand and celebrated all over the world. He shows how, from the beginning of their encounters with Europeans, the Maori adapted outside influences, whether it was modern firearms or the new religion of Christianity, and produced fascinating hybrid work that ranged from elaborately carved rifle butts to a Madonna and Child statue adorned with the Ta Moko, the sacred Maori facial tattoo(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: With chainsaws, adzes and more than 30 different chisels, master Maori carver Logan Okiwi Shipgood crafts a beautiful 6ft tall 'pou' statue from native New Zealand timber(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: Following Hawaiian artist Dalani Tanahy as he creates a traditional hula skirt from tree bark, symbolising the resurgence of indigenous pride and identity(n)

programme.title: Ovid: The Poet and the Emperor
programme.desc: Michael Wood explores the life, works and influence of the Roman poet and storyteller, who died 2,000 years ago this year. He became famous thanks to Metamorphoses, a compendium of the great tales of Greek myth - but then came scandal and exile. Simon Russell Beale and Anna Friel bring the poet's words to life(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox considers whether the building blocks of life might be found on other planets by studying the required substance that is most abundant on Earth - water. He descends to the bottom of the Pacific to discover the creatures that have evolved in this most alien of environments, and flies above the American Scablands to see the effect of the geological changes caused by water in exactly the circumstances astronomers believe occurred on Mars(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s
programme.desc: Archive performances by groups and solo artists from one of the most energetic and exciting decades of popular music(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as St Catherine's College, Oxford take on Peterhouse, Cambridge, with contestants including Guardian drama critic Michael Billington, novelist Susie Boyt, politician Michael Howard and Coast presenter Mark Horton(n)

programme.title: Barbra Streisand: Becoming an Icon 1942-1984
programme.desc: Actress and singer Barbra Streisand grew up in working-class Brooklyn, dreaming of escape from her tough childhood. A stellar student, she resisted the pressure to go to college as her sights were firmly set on Broadway. She was determined to become an actress and landed her first role aged 16, but it was two years later, when she started to sing, that her career took off. This documentary charts her rise to stardom and the remarkable achievements of her early career(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Mark Goodier present the edition first aired on December 15 featuring performances by Bon Jovi, Petula Clark, U2, New Order, the Four Tops, Kim Wilde, Londonbeat, Erasure, Inner City, Cliff Richard and a-ha(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the musical art form from 1973 to 1983, when Dolly Parton crossed over to mainstream success and became the most famous woman in country music. George Jones and Tammy Wynette seemingly lived out their songs' tragic lyrics, while Hank Williams Jr. emerged from his father's shadow and debuted on the Opry show at just 11 years old. Contributors include Dolly Parton, Kris Kristofferson, Emmylou Harris, Willie Nelson, Brenda Lee and Hank Williams Jr. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form from the mid-1970s to the early 1980s. Though no longer heard on country radio, Bluegrass retained its strong core of avid fans. During the same period, Willie Nelson discovered a new music scene in Austin, Texas where hippies and rednecks seemed to get along. Ricky Skaggs had deep Bluegrass credentials, but his time with Emmylou Harris's Hot Band inspired him to experiment with a sound combining the acoustic instruments of a string band with something more electric. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Sisters in Country: Dolly, Linda and Emmylou
programme.desc: How Dolly Parton, Linda Ronstadt and Emmylou Harris's careers took off in the 1970s with very distinct takes on country music, before they came together to collaborate on a successful album in 1987. The artists talk about uniting as harmony singers, focusing on how their alliance made them pioneers in bringing different music worlds together and raising the game for women in the country tradition(n)

programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original(n)

programme.title: Bros: After the Screaming Stops (2018)
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s Bros were one of the biggest bands in the world. Having sold out stadiums, they were the youngest to this day to play Wembley Stadium and their Push album went to Number 1 in more than 20 countries, selling 10 million copies worldwide. After three years of hysteria wherever twins Matt and Luke Goss went, this documentary recalls the aftermath - the fragility of fame and the heartbreak that followed. It also charts the pair's professional reunion 28 years on, having hardly spoken and not played together since their split, as they prepare to play London's O2(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: Suzy Klein explores the use, abuse and manipulation of music in the Second World War - from swinging jazz to film soundtracks and from mushy ballads to madcap ballets. The war, she demonstrates, wasn't just a military fight but an ideological battle where both sides used music as a weapon to secure their vision for civilization(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe sifts through the Top of the Pops archives to present a selection of festive performances, including songs by Slade, the Pogues, Elton John, Take That, Coldplay and Abba(n)

programme.title: What We Were Watching: Christmas 1988
programme.desc: Grace Dent looks at the television shows that Britain's viewers were watching over the Christmas period of 1988, focussing on that week's TV schedules to explore how TV offerings change over the festive season(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Julian has gone missing from the foster home and Harry is convinced he has been abducted. When police search Mosswood, Vera is adamant that she is not responsible. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Harry discovers that an eyewitness saw a man arguing with Marin outside the Five Nations motel room, but that Julian was not with her. Brick calls Harry with an important lead that was found during the raid on Mosswood. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Bob Monkhouse
programme.desc: The story of the comedian and game show host's life and career, told through the archive of films, TV shows, letters and memorabilia that he left behind after his death in 2003. Contributors include Ronnie Corbett, Barry Cryer, Michael Grade and Lenny Henry(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: DW gets jealous when Grandma Thora becomes the most popular babysitter in town(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis and the gang think they have caused Ralf to lose his job(n)

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When an evil version of Danger Mouse escapes from a parallel dimension and joins forces with Baron Greenback, the hero may have finally met his match(n)

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Mum and Dad go out, leaving Boy in charge of the house(n)

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Oscar tells Olive about his early days at Odd Squad(n)

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Enthusiastic young cooks take part in a culinary competition to determine which of them is Britain's best junior baker(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Dillon and Liam assert themselves in different ways. Will they stand up for what's right?(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Zoe rises to a new challenge, but how will she react when her dream is derailed?(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Troubled Eric refuses to play in the U12's next game. Can Molly uncover his secret?(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Drama about a gifted schoolboy footballer, whose parents have recently split forcing him to move house and school. Louis Dunn stars(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Only one team can go to Sweden, but will Dillon be able to hold it together?(n)

programme.title: Jamie Johnson

The main focus is Brexit, and what changes are most likely for the United Kingdom in terms of its future relationship with the European Union. programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A keeper at Longleat House and Safari Park discovers a concerning abscess on one of her red pandas. After antibiotics and painkillers fail to help the growth, Koda needs an urgent visit to the vet for an operation. Presented by Ben Fogle and Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Sports Personality of the Year edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Ama Agbeze, Greg Rusedski, Eddie Izzard, Hannah Cockcroft, Rebecca Adlington, Michael O'Neill, Jayne Torvill, Robin Cousins and Geraint Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are mother and daughter Tracey and Drea from High Wycombe, married couple Daniel and Rebecca from Aberdare, and brothers Karl and Ivan from Manchester. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees the remaining couples - Kelvin Fletcher and Oti Mabuse, Emma Barton and Anton Du Beke, and Karim Zeroual and Amy Dowden - perform a routine of their own choosing, one set by the judges and one last show dance. As usual Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse will be reviewing their efforts, but only the viewers' votes will count to determine the winner. There is also a performance by pop superstar Taylor Swift(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Ellie Goulding hands Michael her phone for Send To All and there is a performance by tenor Andrea Bocelli. Plus, two brothers from Coventry visit a relaxing spa, but get a shock when they find themselves on stage at the London Palladium as they become the Unexpected Stars of the Show(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Watford at Anfield and Leicester City v Norwich City at the King Power Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's Reds held an extremely strong recent record over the Hornets, with their last three meetings on this ground finishing 5-0, 5-0 and 6-1. The home fans will have been hoping for more of the same to maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title. Plus, action from Burnley v Newcastle United at Turf Moor, Chelsea v Bournemouth at Stamford Bridge, Sheffield United v Aston Villa at Bramall Lane and Southampton v West Ham United at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's AFC game - Baltimore Ravens v New York Jets at M&T Bank Stadium(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The keepers are building a new enclosure for some of the furriest mammals - but there is a lot to do if they are to get them into their new home in time for Christmas(n)

programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits property developments in Wolverhampton and Croydon and Martin Roberts looks round three-bedroom semi-detached in Derby(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah goes property shopping in rural Devon with a couple who have a £350,000 budget and hope to find a home that has both a workshop and an art studio(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers meet a couple in Newport, Gwent, whose garden boasts dangerous sloped banks full of wild weeds, overgrown hedges and too many trees(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Having finally discovered the truth, Whitney finds herself in a dangerous situation, so Tiffany and Callum spring into action in an attempt to bring her to safety - will they be able to get to her in time? Ruby tells Max she is fed up of Ian - and decides it is time to teach the pompous restaurant owner a lesson. Jay offers Stuart a job and does his best to make it up to Lola(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Halloween presents one of the last big sales opportunities of the year, so the pressure is on Emma and Martin from the produce team, battling the wet weather to ensure this year's pumpkin crop will deliver the one million required for stores around the country. In the Locksbottom branch in Kent, Halloween could end up being a bit of a nightmare. The store has a low customer satisfaction rating, so manager Mij is on a mission to retrain and re-invigorate his young team in preparation for the big pumpkin push. Meanwhile, Laura and Donna from the communications team head to Romania for the filming of Sainsbury's Dickensian-themed Christmas advert. How will it stand up against the commercials produced by their rivals?(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Glenn Campbell presents live coverage as the General Election results are announced, with reports from every count across the country and the key moments throughout the rest of the UK. Brian Taylor provides analysis, Laura Miller maps out the story with state-of-the-art graphics, Rebecca Curran has the latest news as the results roll in and Martin Geissler gets the reaction to the big stories from key figures(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Glenn Campbell presents live coverage as the General Election results are announced, with reports from every count across the country and the key moments throughout the rest of the UK. Brian Taylor provides analysis, Laura Miller maps out the story with state-of-the-art graphics, Rebecca Curran has the latest news as the results roll in and Martin Geissler gets the reaction to the big stories from key figures(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Glenn Campbell presents live coverage as the General Election results are announced, with reports from every count across the country and the key moments throughout the rest of the UK. programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Emily Maitlis presents from Westminster, with all the results and reaction from the 2019 General Election so far. Clive Myrie reports from Downing Street as the UK finds out who'll be its next Prime Minister, while Victoria Derbyshire talks to voters in Birmingham, Christian Fraser and John Curtice analyse the results, and Louise Minchin presents the latest election news from across the country(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a London couple on their country house hunt in Wiltshire who need their generous £1million budget to afford them space for their prized possessions - a baby grand piano and garaging for classic cars. Away from a positive property search, Nicki discovers more about the art of wooden hat block-making, a critically endangered heritage craft being kept alive by a Wiltshire based family whose blocks are used by milliners across the world(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycler Sarah Peterson's creativity is tested with a tired set of cane chairs, while robot maker Mark Haig's challenge is to come up with a new use for an old video projector. For his own project, presenter Jay Blades gets crafty with a collection of discarded windows(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Northampton, Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to win the right to make over the garden of Theresa and Martyn. Theresa has recently retired from a career in nursing and Martyn has retired from a long teaching career. So, after being married for 42 years, and with their children moved out and providing them with grand children, they are free to enjoy their new 'forever home'. However, their garden is presently 'boring' and 'too green'(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life as the team takes on a broken reel-to-reel tape recording machine, a costume that once belonged to a 1950s strong woman and a wind-up car that's gone 'kaput'. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Two hosts round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Test Drive
programme.desc: Grado hosts a car-based game show in which three teams from Lanark answer questions via a specially-adapted satnav that will determine their route to a well-known tourist attraction. Correct responses mean they will follow the shortest route, but if they are wrong there will be diversions, wrong turns and miles added to their trip(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian prepares for his campaign presentation - while Ruby gets ready to commit sabotage. Jay regrets giving Stuart a trial at the undertaker's when he suggests a Christmas promotion to drum up business. Rainie arrives back in Walford and seeks out Kathy to apologise for everything(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: As the dust settles after the first winter General Election in decades, Fiona Bruce is joined in London by guests from across the political spectrum to ascertain what changes the outcome is likely to bring. The main focus is Brexit, and what changes are most likely for the United Kingdom in terms of its future relationship with the European Union. However, the campaign also turned up a lot of other issues regarding the NHS, public spending and defence, and all these matters are likely to be debated in this special edition(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedy quiz show, with panellists joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winners Judi Dench and Jennifer Hudson talk about the new film version of Cats, Hollywood stars Hugh Grant and Matthew McConaughey discuss their new action thriller The Gentlemen and everyone's favourite crooner Michael Buble also drops in for a chat. Plus, Coldplay offer an exclusive performance from their new album Everyday Life and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England midfielder Jill Scott, Olympic Taekwondo silver medallist Lutalo Muhammad, rugby league great Kevin Sinfield and Great Britain 400m runner Emily Diamond join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: An event at Claire's work sees Fleabag's efforts to help out inevitably end in disaster, but an introduction to one of her sister's colleagues proves intriguing, while a chance meeting with a stranger is an eye-opener. Comedy written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge as a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living as she spins through London grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water. With Andrew Scott and Sian Clifford and a guest appearance by Kristin Scott Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The remaining candidates head to 70 St Mary Axe - also known as the Can of Ham, a spectacular new 21-storey building in the heart of the City of London. There, they are interviewed by some of Alan Sugar's most trusted advisers, who are primed to pick apart their business plans. There's tough talking, turmoil and tears as bravado breaks, egos are eradicated and business plans bulldozed, before Alan decides which two candidates have done enough to earn a place in the final(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Sabrina Ghayour and David Munoz, while guest Alice Levine faces her food heaven or hell. Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Calvin, Lesley, Don and Suzanne - colleagues from The Old Mart Resource Centre in Maud, Aberdeenshire - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v Watford at Anfield in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Doddie Weir: One More Try
programme.desc: In 2016, Scottish rugby legend Doddie Weir was diagnosed with motor neurone disease. Doctors said he would be in a wheelchair within a year, but instead Doddie has battled the disease and is on a mission to help find a cure. Fellow player John Beattie has followed Doddie and his family over the past two years, gaining a unique insight into the life and mission of the man behind the famous tartan suit(n)

Along the way, the presenter meets a Devon stovemaker and finds out the practical and environmental benefits of this popular country home interior feature(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A keeper at Longleat House and Safari Park discovers a concerning abscess on one of her red pandas. After antibiotics and painkillers fail to help the growth, Koda needs an urgent visit to the vet for an operation. Presented by Ben Fogle and Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Sports Personality of the Year edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Ama Agbeze, Greg Rusedski, Eddie Izzard, Hannah Cockcroft, Rebecca Adlington, Michael O'Neill, Jayne Torvill, Robin Cousins and Geraint Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are mother and daughter Tracey and Drea from High Wycombe, married couple Daniel and Rebecca from Aberdare, and brothers Karl and Ivan from Manchester. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees the remaining couples - Kelvin Fletcher and Oti Mabuse, Emma Barton and Anton Du Beke, and Karim Zeroual and Amy Dowden - perform a routine of their own choosing, one set by the judges and one last show dance. As usual Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse will be reviewing their efforts, but only the viewers' votes will count to determine the winner. There is also a performance by pop superstar Taylor Swift(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Ellie Goulding hands Michael her phone for Send To All and there is a performance by tenor Andrea Bocelli. Plus, two brothers from Coventry visit a relaxing spa, but get a shock when they find themselves on stage at the London Palladium as they become the Unexpected Stars of the Show(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Watford at Anfield and Leicester City v Norwich City at the King Power Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's Reds held an extremely strong recent record over the Hornets, with their last three meetings on this ground finishing 5-0, 5-0 and 6-1. The home fans will have been hoping for more of the same to maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title. Plus, action from Burnley v Newcastle United at Turf Moor, Chelsea v Bournemouth at Stamford Bridge, Sheffield United v Aston Villa at Bramall Lane and Southampton v West Ham United at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's AFC game - Baltimore Ravens v New York Jets at M&T Bank Stadium(n)

programme.title: Election 2019 - Northern Ireland
programme.desc: As the polls close and the overnight count begins, Mark Carruthers and a panel of political experts analyse the results from Northern Ireland's 18 Westminster constituencies as well as the emerging political picture across England, Scotland and Wales. Tara Mills is live with the main players at the Belfast count centre and there will be updates on all the twists and turns of the night from reporters at every count across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: Huw Edwards takes the helm for the BBC's comprehensive overnight coverage as the results of the General Election are announced, joined by Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Faisal Islam and Amol Rajan, along with Professor John Curtice. Sophie Raworth analyses the results on a giant map of the UK, Sarah Smith and Kirsty Wark broadcast live from Scotland, and correspondents including Naga Munchetty, Andrew Marr, Martha Kearney, Nick Robinson and Lucy Manning will have news and results from key constituency locations across the country. Finally, the exit poll - and the eventual result - will be projected onto Old Broadcasting House, providing a clear idea of who will be leading the country over the next five years(n)

programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. programme.desc: Part one. In a Prom that celebrates the history and evolution of the folk music scene in Britain and Ireland, the BBC Concert Orchestra collaborates with Julie Fowlis, the Unthanks, Sam Lee and Jarlath Henderson who are pushing the boundaries of folk music(n)

programme.title: Blas
programme.desc: Highlights from the 2018 Blas Music Festival, featuring Mischa MacPherson(n)

programme.title: Luingean Lannsaireachd (Surgery Ships)
programme.desc: Mercy Ships' volunteers are faced with an ebola scare. However, surgeons give new hope to a young girl with extremely bowed legs due to malnutrition, a teenage boy with a burns injury so severe his toes don't touch the ground, and a local paediatrician in need of novel and complex surgery so he can get back to work and continue to save the lives of his people(n)

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Aberdeen v Hamilton Academical. A chance to see the top-flight clash from Pittodrie(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news, entertainment and lifestyle features. Martin and Shirlie Kemp talks about their new album In the Swing of It, which marks the first time the couple have worked together, having both enjoyed individual chart success, in Spandau Ballet and Pepsi and Shirlie. The pair talk about their new work and their children's careers(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the dealers are in Warwick, where Stewart Hofgartner makes his oddest purchase yet, and Fay Rutter and Henry Nicholls both bid for paintings(n)

programme.title: The Switch
programme.desc: Quiz in which players can accumulate big cash totals by answering super quick-fire easy money questions. However, in order to take home that cash, they must survive The Switch, a series of three super hard multiple-choice questions that each has only one incorrect choice. Hosted by Sanjeev Bhaskar(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron steps up his campaign, Jai is intent on making Jimmy pay, and Andrea puts on a front(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The average UK household spends an extra £800 during December. But as millions of shoppers are preparing for the festive season, so too are criminals with a whole range of fakes, cons and scams. Adam Shaw investigates how people can get the best value gifts and keep their money safe this Christmas(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron's anger gets out of control, Billy and Al square up to each other, and Leyla feels a pang of jealousy(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo begins this week in the Italian food capital of Bologna, where he picks up a snack for his journey - the Bolognese classic panino with mortadella. He then boards the train and heads towards the rice growing region of Mantova, where he helps out in the paddies and makes a chocolate rice pudding. He then visits Verona, home of one of the country's oldest art schools. Here he models for the students in their life-drawing class before serving up meatballs in tomato sauce for the young artists(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Just a few days after the new King or Queen of the jungle was crowned, Ant and Dec present the usual round-up of what happened behind the scenes as each campmate was voted out. From the first to the last, the programme reveals what they get up to in their first few days of freedom, and captures their reactions to old rivals and trusted friends, while their families gather for emotional reunions with their loved ones(n)

programme.title: Election 2019 Live
programme.desc: As the polls close, News at Ten anchor Tom Bradby presents live through-the-night coverage of the battle for Number 10, revealing who will lead the country for the next five years. He is joined in the studio by his ITV colleagues and two political analysts - Professor Jane Green, co-director of the British Election Study, and election analyst Professor Colin Rallings. Regional correspondents report from the key constituencies as the votes are counted, with commentators in the studio sharing their interpretation of the results as they happen - and what happens next(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring full analysis of the Election results so far, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Election 2019 Live
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham presents a special programme offering analysis of the Election results so far and a prediction of the likely shape of the Government(n)

programme.desc: Staffordshire County Showground plays host to David Dickinson and his dealers. A pair of horse prints have Karen Dalmeny in tears, Stewart Hofgartner goes 'pirate crazy' for a porthole, while Mark Stevens gets hot under the collar when some saucy films come into the den(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rhona is shaken by Graham's actions, Belle is annoyed that Ellis returned from Dubai and did not let her know he was back in the village, and Laurel is caught in the middle(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Robert has been living a double life for months now, but Friday 13th looks like being especially unlucky for the Weatherfield chef as evidence begins to pile up against him. Elsewhere, Tim springs a surprise proposal on Sally, and new parents Gemma and Chesney bring their babies home(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: The Band
programme.desc: The third in this new four-part talent show in which Simon Cowell searches for the next big singing group, hoping he can find a band to follow in the footsteps of previous X Factor success stories as One Direction, Fifth Harmony and Little Mix. Groups discovered on The X Factor have sold more than 400 million records. The winner of this show will be signed to Cowell's record label - but will they prove just as successful as their X Factor predecessors?(n)

programme.title: The Royal Variety Performance
programme.desc: Rob Beckett and Romesh Ranganathan host the annual entertainment extravaganza from the London Palladium, attended this year by the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge. This year's show features music from Robbie Williams, Jamie Cullum, Emeli Sande, Lewis Capaldi, the Kanneh Masons and Mabel, as well as this year's Britain's Got Talent winner, Colin Thackery, and comedy from Frank Skinner, Kerry Godliman and Flo & Joan. There are also appearances from the casts of West End shows Groan Ups, Mary Poppins and Come from Away, and dance spectacle from Zurcaroh and Cirque du Soleil(n)

programme.title: Meet the Parents
programme.desc: Dating show, hosted by Holly Willoughby, which puts the love lives of singletons in the hands of the people they know best - their mum and dad. Each week, one man and one woman must choose from three potential partners, with their decisions solely based on an encounter with the contestants' parents(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Animated comedy adventure series featuring a gang of heroes who protect the city of Technopolis and its King Kenric against evil elements(n)

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Animated comedy adventure series featuring a gang of heroes who protect the city of Technopolis and its King Kenric against evil elements(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean is determined to make the best cup of java, ever. Oh, and to get rich brewing it.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Gran needs new teeth. Probably not wise to nab a set found on the side of the road though, under a full moon.(n)

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Monsters and ghouls get in the way of the Scooby gang's adventures as they travel and spend time together in the summer(n)

programme.title: Mighty Magiswords!
programme.desc: Vambre and Prohyas return to Adventure Academy where Professor Cyrus sends them on a quest to find the Legendary Knowledge Magisword.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The chef is back in the kitchen in the run-up to Christmas with festive food and guests including Johnny Vegas, Aled Jones, chef Paul Ainsworth and Nicola Lando. Plus, wine guru Oz Clarke is taste-testing the best wines on supermarket shelves(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner create a crowd-pleasing menu, including vol-au-vents, baked camembert with cheese straws and mince pies(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha and Nisha Katona create a family feast from India, including tea-steeped chickpeas with black cardamom rice for breakfast, and gingerbread lamb raan for dinner(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo begins this week in the Italian food capital of Bologna, where he picks up a snack for his journey - the Bolognese classic panino with mortadella. He then boards the train and heads towards the rice growing region of Mantova, where he helps out in the paddies and makes a chocolate rice pudding. He then visits Verona, home of one of the country's oldest art schools. Here he models for the students in their life-drawing class before serving up meatballs in tomato sauce for the young artists(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a one-off 1980s edition of the dating game. A group of men enters the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Angellica Bell, Oritse Williams, Leslie Ash and Ian Lavender pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story (2016)
programme.desc: In a galaxy far, far away, Jyn Erso - the maverick daughter of scientist Galen Erso - joins the Rebel Alliance. On the planet Scarif, she and the freedom fighters, including a blind warrior and a deadpan droid, attempt to steal plans for the Death Star, a battle station capable of destroying entire worlds, which was designed by her father on behalf of the Empire. However, the Rebels face tough competition from Imperial warlord Orson Krennic and shadowy forces, including Darth Vader, who will come to play a major part in the rise of the Empire. Star Wars prequel set before the events of A New Hope, starring Felicity Jones, Diego Luna and Ben Mendelsohn(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined on the sofa by Cats stars Rebel Wilson and Jason Derulo, promoting the big-screen version of the famous Andrew Lloyd Webber musical. World Cup-winning England cricketer Jofra Archer, Rocketman actor Taron Egerton and former Bake Off presenter Mel Giedroyc also drop by, and there is music by Keane(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents a special edition of the matchmaking show, celebrating its tenth series and 10 years since the first edition of its parent show, Taken Out, aired in Australia. In a twist on the usual format, Paddy gives 30 men who have previously appeared on the show a chance to take a seat 'behind the lights', while a selection of female celebrities replace the boys in the 'Love Lift'. Among the stars taking part are The Only Way is Essex star Chloe Sims, and Olympic gymnast Claudia Fragapane. The host also catches up with some of the couples who found lasting love after meeting on the show(n)

programme.title: Tim Burton's The Nightmare Before Christmas (1993)
programme.desc: Jack Skellington, the Pumpkin King of Halloween Town, tires of being scary and decides to take over the friendly world of Christmas. Stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Chris Sarandon, Catherine O'Hara and Paul Reubens, and a musical score by Danny Elfman(n)

programme.desc: Brian seeks out old flame Tracy, afraid he may have lost his last chance at love. To his surprise, she reveals he fathered her son Dylan, and drops the boy off at the Griffins' house. programme.desc: Ten interlinked stories of people searching for and finding love. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Griffins are stuck indoors during a blackout, so Peter passes the time by telling a story spoofing Star Wars. programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective delves into the past of extreme sports enthusiast Gideon Frane after he plunges from a cliff in a ball of flames. Clare Balding has the results as they happen, Matt Forde will run the Alternative News Desk and Rob Rinder is out and about assessing the mood on the ground. Throughout the night the presenters are joined by a mixture of political figures and satirists - including ex-Home Secretary Amber Rudd, former Labour Deputy Leader Tom Watson and comedians Tez Ilyas and Sophie Willan - adding a comedic slant to the analysis. programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: The annual Gala Festival is staged, showcasing steam engines from up and down the country. As part of the events, the North York Moors Railway has agreed to swap its precious 100-year-old Q6 engine, the last example in the world, with an engine from the Severn Valley heritage railway. However, there is a problem as the Q6 is undergoing a two-year overhaul and is not ready. A crack has been found in the engine box, and there is no specialist steam copper welder available at short notice to fix it. If the Q6 cannot be sent to the Severn Valley, the entire festival may be ruined(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild UK
programme.desc: Ben meets 40-year-old Amanda Owen from Huddersfield, who left behind a comfortable existence to pursue her lifelong dream of becoming a hill shepherdess. Despite having no background in farming, she now lives at a 2,000-acre farm in the Yorkshire Dales with husband Clive and their eight children. Ben is given a taste of life as a hill shepherd, learns the highs and lows of farming life and finds out how she copes looking after such a huge family as well as her 1,000 native Swaledale sheep(n)

programme.title: Make a Wish for Christmas

Other stories include Madonna's off-colour tribute to Aretha Franklin at the VMAs and the most shocking moments from Celebrity Big Brother. Plus, the tense stand-off between Kim Woodburn and Coleen Nolan on Loose Women, President Trump's controversial UK visit and claims concerning David and Victoria Beckham's marriage. Featuring contributions by Louis Walsh, Jeremy Vine, Michelle Visage, Jermaine Pennant, Dan Osborne, India Willoughby and Courtney Act(n)

programme.desc: Jon Bentley, Ortis Deley and Georgie Barrat go to the aid of a young family to see which tech for toddlers will come out on top. Jon also tests some budget tablets and laptops, ascertaining whether a mere £250 can secure enough grunt to power through a selection of normal daily tasks. Ortis turns locksmith for a day as he finds out how safe new smart locks are, while Jordan Erica Webber gives presenter Craig Charles a first look at Google's new gaming service(n)

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: A look at the planet's most iconic hotels, beginning with Caesars Palace, a hotel themed on ancient Rome and containing numerous restaurants and casinos, which has been the heart and soul of Las Vegas for more than 50 years. Cameras follow the staff who keep make this 4,000-room hotel running 24 hours a day, go behind scenes of a high roller's secret luxury villa and reveal how the needs of 3,000 people are catered for per day. Plus, the secrets of the casino floor are revealed as a young couple tie the knot Vegas-style(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Starting in Budapest, Jane boards a steam train built at the peak of the Austro-Hungarian Empire for the exclusive use of the emperor and empress. It is not long before she is back on water though, boarding a luxury river ship to sail to the blossoming post-communist cities of Bratislava and Belgrade. From there, Jane heads towards one of the natural highlights of the Danube, the Iron Gates. After coming face to face with folkloric monsters and sampling the local moonshine, she finishes her journey in Romania's most popular destination, Bran Castle, the assumed home of Dracula(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5: Christmas in Lapland
programme.desc: Celebrities Christopher Biggins, Joe Pasquale, the Rev Richard Coles, Rustie Lee and Sue Holderness head out on a festive adventure across the Arctic Circle, offering an insight into the area's culture and history. The group starts its journey in Rovaniemi, the capital of Lapland, before venturing into a vast expanse of uninhabitable wilderness of forest and frozen lakes. As the five celebrities endeavour to get as much out of this four-day break as they can, they also reconnect with memories from their own childhood and discover what Christmas really means to them(n)

programme.title: Craziest Christmas Lights
programme.desc: Noddy Holder narrates this documentary following the extensive festive illuminations of various households. In Bristol, builder brothers Lee and Paul take over their childhood home to put on one of the West Country's biggest extravaganzas - something they have been doing now for 25 years. programme.desc: Seven youngsters discover an old map and set out on the adventure of a lifetime to save their homes from a land developer by finding a legendary pirate's hidden treasure.
